title: Town of Blacksburg Home Occupation Use and Design Standards
excerpt: Home occupation standards restrict outside storage and hazardous materials, limit home occupation floor area to 25% of the dwelling, allow one nonresident worker, prohibit on-site advertising signs, and require traffic volumes consistent with nearby dwellings without adding parking spaces during the home occupation.

title: Town of Blacksburg Zoning Guidance for Business Licenses and Home Occupations
excerpt: The Town instructs prospective businesses to confirm that zoning allows the proposed business use before applying for a business license. The Town's zoning page states that home occupations, meaning businesses run administratively out of the home, are allowed in every Blacksburg zoning district subject to the Home Occupation Application ordinance standards.

title: Blacksburg Zoning Ordinance Off-Street Parking Schedule for Home Occupations
excerpt: The Blacksburg Zoning Ordinance off-street parking schedule lists Home occupation under residential use types and points minimum parking review to section 4211(B)(8), with no maximum parking standard stated in the schedule.

title: Town of Blacksburg Planning and Building Department Building Safety Review
excerpt: The Building Safety Division reviews construction plans for conformance with the Virginia Uniform Statewide Building Code and town ordinances, issues construction permits, inspects projects for approved-plan compliance, and issues certificates of occupancy.

title: Virginia Department of Health Food Establishment Permit Guidance
excerpt: VDH states that foodservice operators opening a new or remodeled foodservice facility should submit plan review materials to the local health department, apply for a Food Establishment Permit before opening, and request a health department inspection before the permit is issued.

title: VDACS Home and Commercial Kitchen-Based Food Businesses
excerpt: VDACS explains that food establishments, including private homes that manufacture, process, pack, or hold food for sale, are subject to Virginia food laws and related regulations unless an exception applies, and operating generally requires inspection and a permit.

title: Montgomery County Home Occupation Zoning Guidance
excerpt: Montgomery County states that home occupations in the unincorporated county are regulated by the zoning ordinance, are permitted in listed residential and agricultural districts, must be secondary to the residence, may not exceed 25% of dwelling floor area, may use an accessory building, and must avoid exterior impacts such as traffic, parking demand, noise, vibration, glare, odors, or electrical interference.

title: Montgomery County Home Occupation Health, Inspections, and Sign Review Triggers
excerpt: The Montgomery County home occupation form directs food-preparation businesses to contact the Health Department about a food service permit, directs projects with additions or structural alterations to contact Montgomery County Inspections, and notes that a home occupation sign requires sign-requirement verification with Planning and a separate permit.

title: Montgomery County Planning and GIS Zoning Permit Review Sequence
excerpt: Montgomery County Planning and GIS Services administers zoning and subdivision ordinances in unincorporated areas. The county directs applicants to submit zoning permit materials to Planning and GIS Services before applying for a building permit at the Department of Inspections, with Planning and GIS listed for zoning questions and Building Inspections listed for building permit questions.
section_ref: Planning and GIS Services; zoning permit approval steps

title: Montgomery County GIS Zoning Map and Parcel Lookup
excerpt: Montgomery County GIS identifies MyGIS OneView as the county's interactive public map resource for parcel, land, and location information, lists a zoning map in the map gallery, and cautions that maps and GIS data are reference materials rather than legal determinations of official county records.
section_ref: GIS and Mapping Services; Map Gallery and disclaimer

title: Montgomery County Residential Building Permit and USBC Compliance
excerpt: Montgomery County's residential building permit application says a permit must be issued before construction begins, applications must include construction plans, all building work must comply with the Virginia Uniform Statewide Building Code, and applicants must contact Planning and GIS Services to discuss zoning and development-code compliance.
section_ref: Residential Building Permit Application; updated April 2025

title: New River Health District Food Establishment Plan Review and Permits
excerpt: The Virginia Department of Health New River Health District describes environmental health inspections for restaurants and other establishments, identifies establishment plan review and permit fees, and provides applications for new or remodeled food establishments, annual permit renewal, mobile food establishments, and temporary food events.
section_ref: Environmental Health; food service establishment applications
